records.ety: [OE. plesen, OF. plaisir, fr. L. placere, akin to placare to reconcile. Cf. Complacent, Placable, Placid, Plea, Plead, Pleasure.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pleased; p. pr. & vb. n. Pleasing.]

defs: To give pleasure to; to excite agreeable sensations or emotions in; to make glad; to gratify; to content; to satisfy.
quotes: I pray to God that it may plesen you.
What next I bring shall please thee, be assured.

defs: To have or take pleasure in; hence, to choose; to wish; to desire; to will.
quotes: Whatsoever the Lord pleased, that did he.
A man doing as he wills, and doing as he pleases, are the same things in common speech.

defs: To be the will or pleasure of; to seem good to; -- used impersonally.
To afford or impart pleasure; to excite agreeable emotions.
quotes: What pleasing scemed, for her now pleases more.
For we that live to please, must please to live.

defs: To have pleasure; to be willing, as a matter of affording pleasure or showing favor; to vouchsafe; to consent.
quotes: Heavenly stranger, please to taste These bounties.
That he would please 8give me my liberty.
